evals.prompt:
Here's a CSV of last month's sales data. Can you generate an executive report from it?

region,product,revenue,units_sold
North,Widget A,12400,310
North,Widget B,8200,205
South,Widget A,9100,228
South,Widget B,15600,390
West,Widget A,7800,195
West,Widget B,6100,153
evals.expected_output: An executive report with: a title (e.g., 'Monthly Sales Report — [Month]'), executive summary (3 sentences highlighting top region/product), Key Metrics table (total revenue: $59,200; total units: 1,481; average revenue per unit), a breakdown table by region and product, Notable Findings (Widget B outsells Widget A in the South; North and West favor Widget A), and Data Notes. Plain language with no jargon. No PII issues in this dataset.

evals.prompt: I exported a CSV of support tickets from this month: ticket_id, status, category, created_at, resolved_at, agent_name. There are 450 tickets. I want a report showing our support team's performance for a team meeting.
evals.expected_output: A report structure asking for the actual data, but also providing the template/approach: Key Metrics (total tickets, resolution rate %, average resolution time), breakdown by category (which categories are most common), breakdown by status (open/closed/pending), breakdown by agent (tickets handled per agent, average resolution time — but no PII leakage in shared versions). If no actual data provided, show the exact template structure with placeholder values and ask user to share the CSV.

evals.prompt: Turn this signup data summary into a report for investors: 243 signups last week, up from 198 the week before. Top sources: organic search (95), social media (72), referral (51), direct (25). Average time to first action: 4.2 minutes. Conversion from signup to paid: 8.2%.
evals.expected_output: A polished investor-focused report with: headline metric (243 signups, +22.7% week-over-week), acquisition source breakdown as a table with percentages, conversion funnel (signups → activation in 4.2 min average → 8.2% paid conversion), Notable Findings (organic search is top channel at 39%, referral has strong conversion intent). Framed positively with appropriate context. Under 300 words total.
